The [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/staticValues] and [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/staticFunctions] arrays are the simplest and most efficient means for vending custom properties. The default [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassRef] that [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassCreate(_:)] creates follows this idiom, instantiating objects with a shared, automatically generating prototype that contains the class’s function objects. # JSClassDefinition

**Structure**

A structure that contains properties and callbacks that define a type of object.

## Declaration

```swift
struct JSClassDefinition
```

## Overview

All fields other than the [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/version] field are optional. Any pointer may be `NULL`.

The [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/staticValues] and [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/staticFunctions] arrays are the simplest and most efficient means for vending custom properties. Statically declared properties automatically service requests like [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/getProperty], [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/setProperty], and [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/getPropertyNames]. Property access callbacks are necessary only to implement unusual properties, like array indexes, which have unknown names at compile time.

If you name your getter function `GetX` and your setter function `SetX`, you declare a [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSStaticValue] array that contains `"X"` like this:

```c
JSStaticValue StaticValueArray[] = { 
    { "X", GetX, SetX, kJSPropertyAttributeNone }, 
    { 0, 0, 0, 0 } 
};
```

Standard JavaScript practice calls for storing function objects in prototypes so you can share them. The default [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassRef] that [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassCreate(_:)] creates follows this idiom, instantiating objects with a shared, automatically generating prototype that contains the class’s function objects. The [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/kJSClassAttributeNoAutomaticPrototype] attribute specifies that a [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassRef] doesn’t automatically generate such a prototype. The resulting [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassRef] instantiates objects with the default object prototype, and gives each instance object its own copy of the class’s function objects.

A `NULL` callback specifies that the default object callback substitutes, except in the case of [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/hasProperty], where it specifies that [doc://com.apple.javascriptcore/documentation/JavaScriptCore/JSClassDefinition/getProperty] substitutes.

## Creating a Class Definition

- **init()**: Creates an empty class definition.
- **init(version:attributes:className:parentClass:staticValues:staticFunctions:initialize:finalize:hasProperty:getProperty:setProperty:deleteProperty:getPropertyNames:callAsFunction:callAsConstructor:hasInstance:convertToType:)**: Creates a class definition with the specified values.
- **JSClassAttributes**: A set of JavaScript class attributes.

## Managing Class Information

- **parentClass**: A JavaScript class to set as the class’s parent class.
- **className**: A null-terminated UTF-8 string that contains the class’s name.
- **version**: The version of the class definition structure.
- **attributes**: A set of class attributes to give to the class.
- **staticValues**: An array that contains the class’s statically declared value properties.
- **JSStaticValue**: A statically declared value property.
- **staticFunctions**: An array that contains the class’s statically declared function properties.
- **JSStaticFunction**: A statically declared function property.

## Managing Callbacks

- **initialize**: The callback for creating the object.
- **JSObjectInitializeCallback**: The callback type for first creating an object.
- **finalize**: The callback for preparing the object for garbage collection.
- **JSObjectFinalizeCallback**: The callback type for finalizing an object (preparing it for garbage collection).
- **hasProperty**: The callback for determining whether an object has a property.
- **JSObjectHasPropertyCallback**: The callback type for determining whether an object has a property.
- **getProperty**: The callback for getting a property’s value.
- **JSObjectGetPropertyCallback**: The callback type for getting a property’s value.
- **setProperty**: The callback for setting a property’s value.
- **JSObjectSetPropertyCallback**: The callback type for setting a property’s value.
- **deleteProperty**: The callback for deleting a property.
- **JSObjectDeletePropertyCallback**: The callback type for deleting a property.
- **getPropertyNames**: The callback for collecting the names of an object’s properties.
- **JSObjectGetPropertyNamesCallback**: The callback type for collecting the names of an object’s properties.
- **callAsFunction**: The callback for calling an object as a function.
- **JSObjectCallAsFunctionCallback**: The callback type for calling an object as a function.
- **hasInstance**: The callback for checking whether an object is an instance of a particular type.
- **JSObjectHasInstanceCallback**: The callback type for checking whether an object is an instance of a particular type.
- **callAsConstructor**: The callback for using an object as a constructor.
- **JSObjectCallAsConstructorCallback**: The callback type for using an object as a constructor.
- **convertToType**: The callback for converting an object to a particular JavaScript type.
- **JSObjectConvertToTypeCallback**: The callback type for converting an object to a particular JavaScript type.

## Working with Classes

- **JSClassCreate(_:)**: Creates a JavaScript class.
- **JSClassRelease(_:)**: Releases a JavaScript class.
- **JSClassRetain(_:)**: Retains a JavaScript class.
- **kJSClassDefinitionEmpty**: A class definition structure of the current version that contains null pointers and has no attributes.
- **JSClassAttribute**: A JavaScript class attribute.

## Conforms To

- BitwiseCopyable
